channel.title: rLLM Project — Blog
channel.description: Research, releases, and updates from the rLLM Project — building the infrastructure to train, evaluate, and evolve intelligent agents.

item.title: Training a Frontier Java Code Migration Agent with AWS AgentCore Runtime
item.dc:creator: AWS AgentCore Science and rLLM Teams
item.description: We train Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B using AgentCore Runtime integration to outperform Claude 4.5 Haiku on long-horizon Java repo migrations.

item.title: Hive: Collaborative Agent Evolution Platform
item.dc:creator: The rLLM Team
item.description: Hive is a collaborative platform for evolving and improving agents together. A swarm of agents iterate on shared tasks, learning from each other to push past what any single agent can reach alone.

item.title: rLLM UI: Real-Time Observability for Agent Training & Evaluation
item.dc:creator: Chanbin Park and the rLLM Team
item.description: A real-time observability platform for training and evaluating agents. Other tools show what is happening during training — rLLM UI shows you why, letting you inspect exactly what the model generates at every step.

item.title: On-Policy Distillation: Training Smaller Students from Stronger Teachers
item.dc:creator: Brian Chen, Kyle Montgomery, and the rLLM Team
item.description: rLLM On-Policy Distillation (OPD) trains smaller students from stronger teachers by using the teacher's policy to guide the student's training — a practical recipe for compact, capable models.

item.title: Faster and Better: Open-Source Recipe for Deep Research Agents
item.dc:creator: rLLM Team
item.description: We achieve 5× faster training (1 day vs 5 days) for deep research agents with rLLM's fully asynchronous architecture, and push accuracy from 30% to 36% on BrowseComp-Plus with a simple test-time document cutoff.

item.title: rLLM-FinQA: A 4B Model that Outperforms 235B and Rivals Gemini 2.5 Pro
item.dc:creator: Manan Roongta, Sijun Tan, Bhavishya Pohani, Charles Dickens, Christopher Glaze
item.description: In a collaboration with Snorkel AI, a domain-specialized 4B model outperforms Qwen3-235B (59.7% vs 51.4%) and performs comparably to Gemini 2.5 Pro (60.6%) on an expert-curated agentic financial benchmark.

item.title: rLLM SDK: Training Any Agentic Program without Code Changes
item.dc:creator: Tianhao Wu, Sijun Tan, and the rLLM team
item.description: The rLLM SDK intercepts LLM calls directly, letting you train any agent framework — LangChain, LangGraph, AutoGen, or custom code — without rewriting for training. What's trainable = what's practical to build.

item.title: rLLM v0.2: RL Training over General Agentic Programs
item.dc:creator: Sijun Tan, Kyle Montgomery, and the rLLM team
item.description: A major upgrade introducing AgentWorkflowEngine and AgentWorkflowTrainer — general abstractions that let you define multi-agent systems and complex workflows, and train them with RL without rewriting production code.

item.title: Pepper: An Event-Driven Architecture for Proactive Agentic Systems
item.dc:creator: Tianhao Wu, Sijun Tan
item.description: Pepper is a real-time, event-driven architecture enabling proactive agentic systems. Our personal assistant proactively fetches and summarizes emails and provides context before you even start a conversation.

item.title: rLLM: Reinforcement Learning for Language Agents
item.dc:creator: Sijun Tan, Michael Luo, Colin Cai
item.description: We release rLLM, an open-source framework for post-training language agents via reinforcement learning. Build custom agents and environments, train them with RL, and deploy them for real-world workloads.
